g.text: Self-improving math reasoning — trains reasoning paths, not just final numbers
Steps look fine but answer is wrong
Answer is right but reasoning is weak
AxiomForgeAI trains against both failure modes
A phase controller decides what the model practices each batch
Phase 1 is grounded-only. Self-play enters only when the model is ready.
Grounded keeps training honest. Self-play adds new challenges when ready.
The model samples several solution paths instead of betting on one answer
Same problem, four different paths — some solid, some shaky
Each attempt is checked from multiple angles simultaneously
GRPO asks which attempt worked best within the group — not in isolation
Scores are normalized within the problem group
The winner is relative — weaker attempts are discounted, not ignored
Stronger reasoning paths become the learning signal
back to phase controller → next batch
Weaker attempts contribute negatively — the gradient learns contrast
Practice → verify → compare → reinforce → adjust → repeat
Each loop makes the model slightly better at both steps and answers
The answer should follow from the reasoning — not just appear at the end
Solution where steps and answer agree
Not just the right number — the right path that leads to it
Goal: reasoning and answer always agree
Not just the right number — the right path that leads to it
